# Gundersen et al. 2021 — Extracted Data

Source: Frontiers in Marine Science, https://doi.org/10.3389/fmars.2021.578629
Citation: Gundersen H, Christie H, Gitmark JK, Andersen GS, Bekkby T, Grue MA,
  Handegard NO, Kile MR, Kvile KØ, Moy FE, Norderhaug KM. Norwegian Kelp Forests:
  Distribution, Biomass and Carbon. Frontiers in Marine Science. 2021;8:578629.

## Files
- `table1_kelp_characteristics_by_ecoregion.csv` — field-measured kelp canopy plant characteristics
- `table4_national_carbon_estimates.csv` — national-scale biomass, area, and carbon/sequestration estimates

## Key National Estimates (Table 4)
- Kelp forest area (0–20 m, sea-urchin adjusted): 5,355 km² (CI: 5,082–5,627)
- Standing biomass (national mean): 10 kg/m² (SE=1.2)
- Carbon standing stock: 2.6 million tonnes C (CI: 2.5–2.7)
- CO₂ equivalents (stock): 9.5 million tonnes (CI: 9.1–9.9)
- Annual carbon export: 1.81 million tonnes C/yr (CI: 1.73–1.89)
- Annual sequestration potential: 0.46 million tonnes C/yr (CI: 0.36–1.12)
- Annual CO₂ sequestration: 1.7 million tonnes CO₂/yr (CI: 1.3–4.1)

## Notes
- Sequestration range is wide because it propagates uncertainty from Krause-Jensen & Duarte 2016
  (25.5% of exported carbon is permanently sequestered, range 23.2–61.6%)
- No ecoregion-level carbon breakdown in the paper; Table 4 is national only
- Ecoregions: Skagerrak, North Sea South, Norwegian Sea South, Norwegian Sea North, Barents Sea
